name: kafka-stream-aggregate-trigger
title: Aggregate Kafka Stream
description: Consumes messages from a Kafka topic, accumulates a numeric field into a stateful window, and fires the flow when the window closes (tumbling) or on every message (sliding). Supports keyed windows, event-time watermarks, late-event DLQ routing, overflow policies, MessageID deduplication, idle-timeout auto-close, and state persistence.

display.description: Fire a flow with an aggregate result when a Kafka stream window closes.

display.name: Kafka Connection
display.description: TIBCO Kafka shared connection providing broker addresses, authentication, and TLS settings.

display.name: Topic
display.description: Kafka topic to consume messages from.

display.name: Consumer Group
display.description: Kafka consumer group ID.

display.name: Initial Offset
display.description: Where to start when no committed offset exists.

display.name: Balance Strategy
display.description: Kafka consumer group rebalance strategy. 'roundrobin' (default) — simple round-robin partition assignment. 'sticky' — minimises partition reassignments on rebalance, recommended when using keyed windows to reduce in-flight state loss. 'range' — assigns contiguous partition ranges per consumer.

display.name: Window Name
display.description: Unique name for this window instance across flow executions (e.g. sensor-5s-sum).

display.name: Window Type
display.description: TumblingTime: fixed time buckets. TumblingCount: fixed event batches. SlidingTime: rolling last-N-seconds. SlidingCount: rolling last-N-events.

display.name: Window Size
display.description: Time-based: milliseconds (e.g. 5000=5s). Count-based: number of events.

display.name: Aggregate Function
display.description: Aggregation function applied to valueField over the window.

display.name: Value Field
display.description: The message field whose numeric value is aggregated.

display.name: Key Field
display.description: When set, independent sub-windows are maintained per unique value of this field (e.g. device_id).

display.name: Event Time Field
display.description: Message field containing the event timestamp (Unix-ms int64 or RFC-3339 string). Leave empty to use wall-clock time.

display.name: Message ID Field
display.description: Field used as a unique event ID for per-window deduplication. Leave empty to disable.

display.name: Allowed Lateness (ms)
display.description: Maximum age in ms beyond the watermark that a late event is still accepted. 0 = reject all late events.

display.name: Max Buffer Size
display.description: Cap on the number of values buffered per window. 0 = unlimited.

display.name: Overflow Policy
display.description: What to do when maxBufferSize is exceeded.

display.name: Idle Timeout (ms)
display.description: Auto-close a sub-window that receives no event for this many ms and emit its partial result. 0 = disabled.

display.name: Max Keys
display.description: Cap on the number of concurrent keyed sub-windows. 0 = unlimited.

display.name: Persist Path
display.description: File path for gob-encoded window state snapshots. Leave empty to disable persistence.

display.name: Persist Every N Messages
display.description: Snapshot state every N messages. 0 = persist only on graceful shutdown.

display.name: Commit Offset on Success Only
display.description: When true (default), the Kafka offset is marked processed only when all handlers complete without error — the message is redelivered on flow failure (at-least-once). When false, the offset is always committed regardless of handler result (at-most-once). Note: for windowed aggregation, redelivery re-adds the value to the window and may cause double-counting if state is not persisted.

display.name: Handler Timeout (ms)
display.description: Maximum time in milliseconds allowed for all handlers to complete for a single message. 0 (default) means no timeout. When exceeded the handler is treated as failed and, if Commit Offset on Success Only is true, the offset is not marked.

display.name: On Schema Error
display.description: Behaviour when a message passes JSON decode but fails schema validation (missing valueField, non-numeric value, window.Add error). 'skip' (default): mark the offset and discard — prevents stalls. 'retry': do not mark the offset; message redelivered after restart/rebalance. Use 'retry' only when the schema error is transient and the consumer is idempotent.

display.name: Event Type
display.description: 'windowClose': fires when the window closes with aggregate result (default). 'lateEvent': fires when a message is rejected as late (for DLQ routing). 'all': fires for both.
